text:
Gama (ガマ, Gama) is one of the Heavens Arena's Floor Masters. He only appears in Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission. Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission 

Appearance

Gama is fat in the chest and belly, but thin in the arms and legs. With his greenish skin, long tongue, and webbed hands, he resembles his namesake, a toad. He has dark brown hair, very short at the sides and longer on the top of his head, combed in a middle parting. His eyes are small, whereas his mouth is rather big. 

Gama's fighting uniform consists of a green sweater with a lighter-colored stripe running from one wrist to the other, up his arms and across his round shoulders, a white shirt worn underneath, white trousers, and white and black sneakers. 

Personality

Not much is known about Gama due to his limited screen time in Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission, but he is mainly depicted as calm, impatient, and perhaps somewhat lazy. 

As he and Amoeba awaited the beginning of the Battle Olympia fights, he complained alongside Amoeba about having to wait for the matches to start, but ultimately did nothing but sit around while the other Floor Masters trained. However, Gama was much less whiny than Amoeba and made less of a fuss. 

Plot

The Last Mission

Gama first appears in Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission entering the arena behind Kanjiru while Cocco introduces the Heavens Arena Battle Olympia and the Floor Masters. 

He is later seen in the training room with Amoeba. While the other Floor Masters are training in preparation for their upcoming fights, Gama and Amoeba are off on the sidelines, with Gama sitting on a bench, seemingly doing nothing. He and Amoeba complain about waiting for the fights to start. 

When Jed initially enters the training room in a hooded robe, Gama appears to be excited. He and the other Floor Masters are then quickly knocked out by Jed and injected with his blood. 

Later on, Gon and Killua get trapped in a room with Gama and eight of the other Floor Masters, who start attacking the two boys under the influence of Jed's blood. After the Floor Masters are knocked down, they get back up and go after the boys again with a zombie-like gait. Gon and Killua manage to escape the room, leaving Gama and the other Floor Masters behind. 

Abilities & Powers

Not much is known about Gama's abilities, but the fact that he was a Floor Master is proof enough of his power and proficiency in Nen. Still, he was defeated single-handedly by Jed. Based on a magazine that Killua was eyeing over and how he was shown fighting later in the movie, he seems to rely on his tongue for battles. 

Magazine Finishers

Shitanamezuri (possibly "Lip Licking"): Gama extends his tongue, perhaps in preparation for an attack.

Trivia

Gama means "toad" in Japanese.
